data.message: Attack-of-opportunity timing: I played Shrivelling (asset, cost 3) while engaged with a Grave-Eater. Playing it provoked the Grave-Eater's AoO, whose Forced effect discards 1 random card from hand. The engine discarded the very Shrivelling I was playing (pc0007) and REFUNDED the 3 resources, so the asset never entered play. Per AHLCG rules, when you play a non-fast asset, the card enters play as the action resolves and the attack of opportunity is resolved immediately AFTER — so a random-hand-discard from the AoO should only be able to hit other cards still in hand, never the asset just played (which is now in play). The engine appears to resolve the AoO's forced discard while the played asset is still in hand, fizzling the play. This wrongly cost me my weapon.
